page: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 server family. It is powered by an ARM64-based Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores with 2 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is not utilized. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This virtual machine does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external persistent storage.

Benchmark results indicate that the D2ps_v5 delivers modest performance across various workloads. In single-core CPU testing via stress-ng, the server achieves an average score of 1822.08, while its multi-core performance is weaker at 3613.58. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 140.47 GB/sec, but uncached large block read and write speeds drop to 23.96 GB/sec and 15.68 GB/sec respectively. Computational tasks such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench image processing fall into the lower performance tiers. However, the server maintains stable throughput for basic operations, achieving 2.74 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 2.74 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ps_v5 offers a budget-friendly entry point into ARM-based cloud computing. Its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without the virtualization overhead of shared-core models, though its overall resource density is low. Due to its limited memory capacity and modest multi-core capabilities, this server is best suited for lightweight workloads. Recommended use cases include basic web serving, development and testing environments, simple database operations, and lightweight background tasks that do not require advanced vector or matrix extensions.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64-based general-purpose virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor and is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower-to-average tiers, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1822.08 and a multi-core score of 3613.58. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 2.74 GB/sec and handles 2.74 million Redis SET operations per second. Given its modest hardware profile and dedicated CPU allocation, the D2ps_v5 is highly cost-efficient for entry-level workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, software development, testing, and basic database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine offering dedicated compute cores and balanced memory for lightweight, cost-efficient general-purpose workloads.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and supports Premium Storage
Delivers 2.74 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
